claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child (or children) you want to claim Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know the age(s) of the child(ren) you're asking about, and if you have multiple children, please share their names and ages so I can assess each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh hi, yes, I'm asking about my son Alex. He's 6 years old and lives with me here at home. I just wanted to check if I can claim Child Benefit for him?

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful, thank you for that information about Alex! A 6-year-old child falls into the "under 16" category, which is great news for eligibility purposes.

Let me continue through the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Since Alex is only 6 years old, he wouldn't be in paid employment. So the answer to whether he's working less than 24 hours per week is clearly **yes**.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question: **Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right?**

Specifically, this refers to benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that the child themselves would be claiming (not benefits you receive as a parent).

For a 6-year-old, this would typically be **no** - children this young don't usually receive these benefits in their own name. Can you confirm that Alex is not receiving ESA or Universal Credit in his own right?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't get any benefits in his own name. He's only 6, so nothing like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's what I expected - thank you for confirming!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Since Alex is 6 years old, he's clearly not in any apprenticeship, so we can move forward.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great progress! Now I need to check your residency status.

**Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if **any one** of these applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. **You have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **You have pre-settled status** and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. **You have pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work (can claim for up to 91 days)
5. **You have pre-settled status** with sufficient financial resources (and not claiming certain means-tested benefits)
6. **You have pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient financial resources
7. **You have pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. **You're a Crown servant** living abroad (or in certain qualifying countries)

You mentioned Alex lives with you "here at home" - can you confirm that you normally live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - I live in the UK. We're based here in England.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you for confirming that you normally live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to confirm your responsibility for Alex.

**Are you responsible for Alex?**

You qualify if **any one** of these applies:

1. **You live with Alex** (you mentioned he lives with you at home, so this sounds like it applies!)
2. **You contribute to his upkeep** - paying at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards his care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, etc.)
3. **You pay maintenance** under a court order or agreement

Since you mentioned earlier that Alex "lives with me here at home," can you confirm that you live with him and are responsible for his care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely - Alex lives here at home with me and I look after him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent, that's clear - you live with Alex and are responsible for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good progress! Now I need to check about other potential claimants.

**Is anyone else currently claiming (or planning to claim) Child Benefit for Alex?**

For example:
- Alex's other parent
- Another family member
- A previous guardian

Child Benefit can only be paid to one person per child, so I need to know if there are any other claims or potential disputes.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
We're nearly there! I just need to check Alex's current living situation.

**Where is Alex currently living?**

- **At home with you** (normal living arrangement)
- **In hospital**
- **In local authority care**
- **In a foster care arrangement**

Since you mentioned Alex lives at home with you, I assume it's a normal living arrangement - is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, actually, he has been in local authority care for a little while now - a couple of months or so. But it's just temporary.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for letting me know - that's an important detail. I need to check a bit more about Alex's care situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. When a child is in local authority care, there are specific rules about Child Benefit eligibility.

You mentioned Alex has been in care for "a couple of months" - I need to ask a couple of follow-up questions:

1. **Has Alex been in care for 8 weeks or less?** (The rules allow Child Benefit to continue for up to 8 weeks when a child is in care)

2. **If it's been longer than 8 weeks**, does Alex spend **at least 24 hours per week at home with you**?

There's also an exception if Alex spends either:
- **2 consecutive nights per week** at home with you, OR
- **7 or more days in a row** at home with you

Could you clarify how long Alex has been in care and how often he comes home to stay with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's been in care for about 7 weeks now. So yes, it's been less than 8 weeks.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's helpful to know - since Alex has been in care for about 7 weeks, which is less than the 8-week limit, you can still qualify for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
One last question:

**Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance while he's in care?**

This is important because if the council is paying for Alex's accommodation or upkeep during his time in care, this would affect your Child Benefit eligibility.

Do you know if the council is covering these costs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying for his accommodation or maintenance. I'm still responsible for all of that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's the final piece of information I needed.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great news! Based on all the information you've provided, I can now give you my assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## 🎉 Good News - You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit for Alex!

Based on everything you've told me, you meet all the requirements to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

### Summary of your situation:
- ✅ Alex is under 16 years old
- ✅ You normally live in the UK
- ✅ You are responsible for Alex
- ✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- ✅ Although Alex is temporarily in local authority care (7 weeks), this is within the 8-week limit
- ✅ The council is not paying for his accommodation or maintenance

---

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** (or adoption certificate if applicable)
4. Payments are typically made **every 4 weeks** (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

🔗 **Apply here:** [https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

---

### ⚠️ Important Note:
If you or a partner earn over **£60,000** per year, you may be subject to the **High Income Child Benefit Charge**. You can still claim, but some or all of the benefit may need to be repaid through your tax return. You can check the details on the GOV.UK website.

Also, please be aware that if Alex remains in care for **longer than 8 weeks**, you may need to reassess your eligibility unless he spends at least 24 hours per week at home with you.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), so qualifies based on age. He is not working, not receiving benefits in his own name, and not in an apprenticeship. The claimant normally lives in the UK and is responsible for Alex (lives with him). No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Although Alex has been in local authority care for about 7 weeks, this is within the 8-week limit during which Child Benefit can continue. The council is not paying for Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. All standard eligibility criteria are met, and although Alex is temporarily in local authority care, the duration (7 weeks) is within the permitted 8-week period, and the council is not paying for his accommodation or maintenance.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Has the child been in care for 8 weeks or less, OR do they spend at least 24 hours a week at home?
response.help_text: Child Benefit continues for up to 8 weeks in care. Exception if child spends 24+ hours/week at home. Can claim again if child spends 2 consecutive nights/week or 7+ days in a row at home.

age_limits.birthday_rule: Benefit stops on child's 20th birthday, not end of academic year

training_exclusions: Training with employer agreement for future job

payment_rates.note: Current rates not specified in source documents - check gov.uk for latest rates

high_income_charge.note: High Income Child Benefit Charge applies if you or partner earns over £60,000. This is a payment adjustment, not an eligibility criterion.

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
